creator: berg2839@umn.edu
text: Currently, freebsd-version is not very useful on -STABLE and -CURRENT since the version information it gives is too basic. freebsd-version on a release version can tell you if the kernel is a different version from the userland and if the running kernel is different from the installed kernel (by comparing to uname output). On non-release versions, there could be some serious mismatches between kernel and userland (especially on -CURRENT), and freebsd-version would not indicate it, nor can it indicate that the userland was successfully updated.
